sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
Chief Complaint
A cough which was diagnosed as bronchitis by his PCP, and a lung nodule discovered on a chest x-ray.
History of Present Illness
The patient reports a cough for approximately three months, initially noticed while loading payables for horses. He describes the onset around the time he contracted a cold. He reports regular biking, including recent use of the new rail trail, and states he does not typically get sick. He reports taking methotrexate seven and a half milligrams weekly for rheumatoid arthritis, which he feels is under control with continued activity. He quit smoking seven years ago and feels better as a result. He denies any current pain or shortness of breath. His mother had breast cancer, and he has no family history of lung cancer or lung infections.
Findings
The patient's vital signs are stable, with blood pressure of 120/76 and a heart rate of 70. Oxygen saturation is 98% on room air. Lung auscultation reveals clear, equal bilateral breath sounds. Examination reveals mild swelling to the knuckles on the third digit of each hand, consistent with rheumatoid arthritis. Pulses are intact and capillary refill is brisk. The chest CT revealed a solitary 2 centimeter nodule in the lateral aspect of the right upper lobe, which appears smooth and without evidence of emphysema.
Assessment

sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
The patient presents with a solitary lung nodule in the right upper lobe, requiring further investigation. He also has a history of rheumatoid arthritis and a history of smoking cessation.
Plan
I am ordering a pulmonary function test to assess lung function. I am also scheduling a PET CT to determine the metabolic activity of the lung nodule. I recommend video assisted thoracoscopy to remove the nodule and a small portion of the lung. I will also place a chest tube and admit the patient for one night. I will follow up with the patient in four weeks if symptoms persist. I will also schedule a follow-up with the patient's rheumatologist to continue his methotrexate therapy.
